questions.question: What are the setback requirements from wetland boundaries?
What are the penalties for unauthorized wetland disturbance?
How do I apply for a wetland permit or variance?
Are there mitigation requirements for permitted wetland impacts?
What types of development are completely prohibited in wetlands?
questions.answer:
The types of development completely prohibited in wetlands, without a permit, include:

1. Any form of draining, dredging, excavation or removal of soil, mud, sand, gravel or other material.
2. Any form of dumping, filling, grading or depositing of any soil, stones, sand, gravel, mud, rubbish or fill of any kind.
3. The erection of any structures or roads, the driving of pilings or the placement of any other obstructions, whether or not changing or diverting the flow of water.
4. The introduction of any form of pollution, including but not limited to installing a septic tank, running a sewer outfall, discharging sewage treatment effluent or other liquid wastes into or so as to drain a freshwater wetlands area.
5. Any other activity which may substantially impair the natural functions served by the wetlands or the benefits derived therefrom.

(See § 49-3 A)

questions.question: Are there tax incentives for wetland preservation?
What monitoring and maintenance requirements apply to permitted wetland projects?
